title: TanStack Start loader transport boundary implementation tasks
principles: Keep schema-encoded Effect outcomes intact through SSR loader dehydration/hydration and client-navigation server-function RPC serialization.
Treat route-root restoration as a narrow transport boundary, not permission for defensive decoding in React.
Encode success or exactly one expected failure with Schema.Exit and Schema.Never; preserve defects and interruptions as failed causes and reject invalid failure cardinality.
Use canonical schemas, schema-derived types, tagged errors, Result, Exit, Cause, Match and linear Effect pipelines.
Keep route loaders on encoded representations and leaf components on focused canonical values.
Forbid route-specific decoder hooks, wrapper components, mirrored transport DTOs and one-use mapper/helper sprawl.
Enforce decoder implementation placement and route-root restoration placement with distinct tested Oxlint policy.
Implement one task at a time with three documented improvement audits and parent acceptance before the next task.
beforeImplementation: Create docs/exec-plans/active/tanstack-start-loader-transport-boundaries.md and add it to the active plan index before runtime or lint edits.
Revalidate the current TaxKit docs route flow and the reference files under ~/Projects/site/apps/web/src/lib/loader before editing.
Review the separate SSR and client-navigation call graphs; recheck bun.lock and installed TanStack Start, TanStack Router and Effect source, then record any API differences before editing.
Use exactly one sequential subagent per task; the parent reviews, audits, verifies and accepts each slice before delegating the next.
Do not run bun run version-repo, publish packages or consume existing Changesets.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit each coherent implementation slice only after mandatory verification, three documented improvement audits, Changeset review or no-Changeset rationale and parent acceptance.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset status --verbose and explicit no-Changeset rationale
evidenceRequired: Current and final SSR, client-navigation, failure-policy and test call graphs reconciled separately against code.
Proof that TanStack loaders return the encoded Schema.Exit representation unchanged.
Proof that Schema.Never plus encodeExit policy prevents defects, interruptions, empty causes and multiple typed failures from becoming recoverable loader data.
Proof that each direct route-root consumer restores once and ordinary components, leaves and hooks do not restore or decode.
Positive and negative real Oxlint CLI evidence for decoder placement, route-root restoration, aliases and inline-disable rejection.
Unit evidence for success, every current tagged expected failure, malformed transport, defect, interruption, composite causes, empty causes and multiple typed failures.
Compile-time evidence that a route boundary rejects programs with mismatched success or error channels.
Vitest Browser Mode evidence for client-side route success, recoverable failure, malformed transport and framework error boundaries without claiming SSR coverage.
Real docs evidence separating initial SSR/hydration from client navigation and its server-function RPC, on the home route and one content route with a clean browser console.

Effect audit showing one linear encode pipeline, Effect.failCause for defects/interruption, Effect.orDie only for canonical codec-encoding invariants, inline typed error mapping, canonical schemas and no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, broad catches or nested runtime execution.
Helper audit showing the one generic route-boundary constructor owns repeated policy, the current one-use toRouteResult helper is removed and no route-specific decoder hooks or wrappers were added.
Browser-safe import audit proving the route-boundary module reaches only the app-local error owner, Effect and browser-safe docs-content schema/error exports.
Changeset status and explicit app-internal/tooling/docs no-Changeset rationale.
Parent acceptance record for every task, including correction turns when required.

tasks.title: Move docs loader outcomes to the real TanStack transport boundary
tasks.goal: Return schema-encoded Effect outcomes from both docs loaders and restore them only at their direct route roots.
scope: Refactor apps/docs/src/lib/docs/route-boundary.ts around Schema.toCodecJson(Schema.Exit(success, error, Schema.Never)).

Move DocsContentPreloadError and DocsRouteTransportError into one canonical browser-safe apps/docs/src/lib/docs/errors.ts module so MDX and route code no longer depend on each other for error ownership.
Keep one policy-owning generic constructor for the home and page contracts; do not extract a package or add per-route wrappers.

Implement encodeExit as one linear Effect pipeline using Effect Array, Match, Exit and Cause: encode success or exactly one expected failure, preserve any defect/interruption with Effect.failCause and convert empty/multiple typed failure causes into explicit invariant defects.

Expose one synchronous restore operation that maps malformed input and decoded defect/interruption/empty/multiple-failure invariants to DocsRouteTransportError, while flattening exactly one expected Exit failure to a typed Result failure without throwing.
Remove the current one-use toRouteResult helper and keep Exit flattening inline in restore; retain only schema-generated operation bindings and helpers that own repeated policy.

Keep Effect.orDie limited to encoding values already constrained by the canonical success/error schemas; expected application errors remain typed and use mapError/catchTag/catchTags only where policy requires refinement.
Change loadDocsHome and loadDocsPage so their return value to TanStack is the encoded representation from the server function, unchanged.
Keep createServerFn strict TypeScript input/output serialisability checks enabled; do not use strict: false, output opt-outs or casts, and leave runtime serializer proof to the browser slice.

Restore one immutable Route.useLoaderData binding exactly once per direct route-component invocation, then match Result in that component before passing focused canonical values to composition or leaves.
Keep current expected-failure presentation, navigation and MDX behaviour unchanged.
Keep extracted leaves or containers free of Route.useLoaderData, route-boundary imports, unknown, decoders, Effect services and runtime execution.

Update focused route-boundary tests for canonical success, every current expected docs failure, malformed transport, standalone and composite defect/interruption causes, empty causes and multiple typed failures.
Add a compile-time fixture proving a boundary rejects programs whose success or error channel does not match its canonical schemas.
Update docs/architecture/effect-services.md and docs/architecture/frontend.md with the corrected TanStack transport lifecycle when the code lands.
Add a correction/follow-up note to docs/product-specs/boundary-only-decoding.md without rewriting its historical implementation evidence.

mandatorySubagentContract: You are implementing exactly one task from the TaxKit spec/task list.
Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*.
Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
Review the spec call-graph diagrams before editing, and update or report them if implementation discovers a different final graph.
MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: Data, Schema, Array, Chunk, HashSet, HashMap, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Service, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, Command and ManagedRuntime.

Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, switch, Object.values, Object.entries, ad hoc _tag objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.

Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use Effect.mapError, Effect.catchTag, Effect.catchAll or Effect.catchAllDefect.

Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe exports instead of importing server-only internals; the route-boundary import graph may use the app-local error owner, Effect and browser-safe docs-content schemas/errors but no MDX client loader, service, runtime, server, filesystem, environment or generated-source module.
Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with bun run changeset. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.
Run this task mandatory verification gates, including bun run verification.
Run and document at least three implementation improvement audit passes before handoff.
Audit helper sprawl, canonical type/schema/error reuse, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, stringly branching and browser-safe imports.
Report changed files, commands, outcomes, residual risks, Changeset impact and whether the final call graph matches the spec.
Do not start or delegate another task. The parent must review and accept this task first.
documentationRequirements: Read and follow docs/standards/documentation-style.md, documentation-writing.md, documentation-templates.md, documentation-templates/reference.md, documentation-review.md, documentation-architecture.md, documentation-user-journeys.md and documentation-user-journeys/new-contributor.md before editing docs.
Use docs/product-specs/writing-specs.md for product-spec structure and the reference template for durable architecture pages.
Use Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, direct technical prose, canonical names and accurate source-of-truth links.
Keep architecture pages durable and the active exec plan focused on implementation evidence.
taskSpecificScope: Implement the target production and failure-policy call graphs for both current docs routes.
Use Schema.Never, Effect.failCause for defect/interruption, Effect Array for exact failure cardinality and typed Result for restored expected/transport states.
Use Effect.orDie only for canonical codec-encoding invariant failures; preserve expected application errors with typed mapError/catchTag/catchTags policy and never broad catch them.
Remove toRouteResult and keep one-off Schema error mapping and Exit flattening inline in restore.

Keep the route boundary component thin: one immutable loader binding, one restore binding, an exhaustive Result match, meaningful composition components and focused pure leaves. Do not forward encoded data or the whole Result to children.
Do not add route-specific hooks, providers, HOCs, memoisation wrappers or duplicate schemas.
Do not change visible docs content or navigation.

mandatoryVerification: bun run test:docs-boundaries
bun run --filter=docs check-types
compile-time mismatch fixture fails if an incompatible success or error program becomes accepted
bun run --filter=docs build
bun run docs:validate
bun run verification
rg audit proving loadDocsHome/loadDocsPage return encoded server-function output without restore/decode

rg/AST audit proving each current docs route root uses one immutable Route.useLoaderData binding, restores once per invocation, matches Result locally and passes neither encoded data nor whole Result to children

Effect audit against docs/architecture/effect-services.md for linear encode flow, exact one-failure cardinality with Effect Array, Effect.failCause for any defect/interruption, Effect.orDie only at canonical codec encoding, typed failures, canonical schemas, no unsafe casts, no broad catches and no nested runtime

helper audit proving the generic constructor owns repeated policy, toRouteResult was removed, no one-use decoder/error wrappers were added and producer/consumer cardinality policy was not unified through unknown, unsafe casts or polymorphic callbacks

import-graph rg audit proving route-boundary.ts imports the neutral app-local error owner rather than the MDX client loader, and imports no docs-content service/runtime/server export, filesystem/environment adapter or generated server source
parent documentation review for reader fit, Australian spelling, sentence-case headings, banned language, template fit, useful diagrams, canonical names/types and link/source-of-truth accuracy
bun run changeset status --verbose and explicit app-internal no-Changeset rationale
git diff --check
qualityAudits: Pass 1: compare implementation separately to the SSR and client-navigation call graphs; remove early restoration, repeated decoding and any route value that bypasses the codec.

Pass 2: improve Effect control flow and error ownership; require the neutral error module, Schema.Never, exact Cause cardinality with Effect Array, Effect.failCause for defect/interruption, inline typed mapping and no unsafe casts or broad catches.

Pass 3: improve React composition and helper shape; keep one immutable loader binding and one restore at route roots, match Result locally, keep leaves focused and pure, and reject wrappers or abstractions that only shorten files, erase output-specific cardinality policy or silence lint.
completionCriteria: Both docs loaders return the schema-encoded representation unchanged to TanStack.
Both server functions retain strict TypeScript input/output serialisability checks without casts or opt-outs.
Both direct route roots use one immutable loader binding, restore once per invocation and match Result before child composition.
Expected errors, malformed transport, standalone/composite defects and interruptions, empty causes and multiple typed failures follow the target policy.
No leaf, ordinary hook or runtime helper owns decoding/restoration, encoded data or the whole route Result.
Parent recorded three audit passes, verified the call graph and accepted the slice before TSLB-002.

tasks.title: Enforce direct route-root transport restoration
tasks.goal: Make the route-root exception statically narrow without weakening repository-wide decoder placement.
scope: Keep taxkit/no-decoding-outside-boundaries globally enabled and keep actual Schema decoder implementation in the exact route-boundary file.
Add taxkit/no-route-transport-restore-outside-consumers for canonical route-boundary restore calls.
Configure exact routeTransportBoundaryModules and routeTransportConsumerFiles entries; reject route globs, filename inference and non-canonical import specifiers.
Allow canonical restore only from direct named imports and only in inline or same-file named createFileRoute component/head consumers receiving that route loader representation.
Fail closed with a specific diagnostic for unresolved route/component bindings and namespace, default, dynamic or CommonJS import shapes that reference canonical restoration.
Reject restore in ordinary components, leaves, hooks, callbacks, helpers, providers and unrelated files, including when colocated in a route file.

Reject every canonical restore-member reference that is not the direct callee of an allowed invocation, including alias/destructuring, computed member, callback passing and call/apply/bind indirection.

Require component restore to consume the direct Route.useLoaderData call or one immutable local binding from it, and require head restore to consume its loaderData input; reject reassignment, multiple restore, getRouteApi, prop, context, closure-capture and child-callback cases.
Reject forwarding the encoded loader value or restored Result to JSX children; the direct consumer must match Result and pass focused canonical values.
Prove that direct Schema decoders in route TSX remain prohibited by the existing rule.
Extend the root boundary-directive lint pass so inline eslint-disable and oxlint-disable comments naming either boundary rule fail.
Use real Oxlint CLI fixtures with --disable-nested-config for allowed and rejected route shapes.
Update docs/architecture/testing-and-quality.md with durable rule behaviour while keeping exact paths only in oxlint.config.ts.
outputs: tools/oxlint/no-decoding-outside-boundaries.test.ts or a focused sibling route-restoration test

taskSpecificScope: Implement and document taxkit/no-route-transport-restore-outside-consumers, enforcing the semantic difference between an actual Schema decoder implementation and a canonical post-hydration restore call.
Do not broad-allowlist route TSX files for all decoder operations.
Do not add route TSX to decodingBoundaryFiles; restoration is governed by the specialised consumer rule while direct Schema decoding stays prohibited.

Read exact boundary-module and consumer-file rule options from oxlint.config.ts; resolve supported inline and same-file named route consumers statically and fail closed on unsupported import or binding shapes.
Treat any canonical restore member reference as invalid unless it is the direct callee of an allowed invocation; do not build speculative arbitrary interprocedural alias analysis.
Do not rely on raw source-text searches for directives or identifiers when AST/comment-token APIs fit.
Keep rule helpers policy-specific and reject speculative generic AST utility extraction.

bun run lint
bun run test
bun run verification
real Oxlint CLI fixtures for allowed direct-call and single-immutable-binding route component restoration
real Oxlint CLI fixture for one allowed statically traceable named route component restore and one rejected unresolved binding
real Oxlint CLI fixture for one allowed route head restore

real Oxlint CLI fixtures rejecting leaf, hook, helper, callback, namespace/default import, alias, destructuring, computed-member, call/apply/bind, reassignment, multiple-restore, getRouteApi, prop, context, closure-capture and Result-to-child cases
real Oxlint CLI fixture proving direct Schema decode in the route file is still rejected
comment-token evidence proving inline disable directives for both boundary rules fail
config audit proving exact routeTransportBoundaryModules/routeTransportConsumerFiles entries and no broad route/package/test glob or ignorePatterns exemption
helper audit for AST utilities, unsafe casts, source-text parsing and duplicated matcher policy

qualityAudits: Pass 1: improve AST coverage and diagnostics; test direct, immutable binding, named, unresolved, import-shape, alias, destructured, computed, callback, call/apply/bind, hook, leaf, component, head and Result-forwarding forms without false positives for unrelated restore methods.
Pass 2: improve configuration narrowness; preserve the global decoder rule, reject whole-route decoder exemptions and prove exact boundary-directive handling through the real CLI.
Pass 3: improve maintainability; remove duplicated visitor logic and speculative helpers while retaining explicit policy ownership and deterministic diagnostic counts.
completionCriteria: taxkit/no-route-transport-restore-outside-consumers allows only exact configured modules/files and the documented direct route-root and head restoration shapes.
Ordinary React components and hooks cannot use route-boundary restoration.
Encoded loader data and restored Result values cannot be forwarded into child composition.
Direct Schema decoding remains prohibited in route TSX.
No broad exemption, nested config or inline-disable loophole exists.
Parent recorded three audit passes and accepted the slice before TSLB-003.

tasks.title: Prove browser behaviour and complete the boundary documentation
tasks.goal: Prove the serialisable loader contract in a real browser lifecycle and leave every canonical document aligned with the final code.
scope: Add app-scoped Vitest Browser Mode and Playwright configuration following the validated ~/Projects/site shape without importing site code.
Keep browser tooling in docs devDependencies and add explicit docs test:browser wiring; do not add production dependencies or production test routes.

Build a programmatic client-side TanStack route harness that consumes encoded loader representations through Route.useLoaderData and the canonical restore operation; do not present this harness as SSR or hydration proof.
Cover success, typed expected failure, malformed transport, defect and interruption; assert expected route UI versus TanStack errorComponent and a clean console.

Run the actual built docs app and verify initial SSR content before interaction, browser hydration without mismatch and client navigation that observes a successful server-function RPC before rendering destination MDX.
Review route composition for pure leaves and remove only abstractions that are unnecessary or exist to satisfy lint; do not perform an unrelated visual refactor.
Re-run the complete decoder and route-restoration inventories and reconcile final call graphs.
Update apps/docs/README.md when local commands or route-boundary guardrails change.
Mark this spec and task list implemented/completed, update the product-spec index and move the execution plan to docs/exec-plans/completed after all gates pass.
Record release status and confirm no package versioning or publication occurred.

Do not extract shared UI or a shared route package from this final proof task.

browserVerification: Run the client-side Vitest Browser Mode route harness in Chromium and prove success, expected failure, malformed transport, defect and interruption behaviour without claiming SSR coverage.
Start the built docs app through its portless docs.taxkit URL, prove server-rendered home content before interaction and then verify hydration retains it.
Navigate in the browser to one real content route, observe a successful server-function request and verify the encoded client-navigation result renders expected MDX content.
Verify there are no hydration mismatch messages, uncaught exceptions or unexpected console warnings/errors.
Capture reviewable browser evidence in the completed execution plan without committing generated screenshots unless repository policy requires them.
qualityAudits: Pass 1: improve behavioural proof; cover all outcome categories through deterministic codec and client-route evidence, then prove initial SSR/hydration and client-navigation RPC separately in the built app without production test routes or brittle assertions.
Pass 2: improve final architecture and component shape; reconcile call graphs, remove early/duplicate restoration, keep leaves pure and reject abstractions that only satisfy lint.
Pass 3: improve documentation and release readiness; reconcile every canonical doc/index/status, verify links and names, confirm no package-facing change and record all final gates.
completionCriteria: The client-side browser harness proves direct route-root restoration and framework error-boundary behaviour without overstating SSR coverage.
The actual built docs app separately proves initial SSR, hydration and client-navigation server-function RPC on real routes with a clean console.
No production test route, unnecessary helper or shared-package extraction remains.
All final code, docs, JSON, build, lint, test, browser and Changeset gates pass.
The spec, task list, indexes and completed execution plan describe the final implementation and release impact accurately.
Parent recorded three final audit passes and accepted the rollout.
